data.title: Coinbase’s 3% Threshold and Texas Law: How New Rules Could Curb Shareholder Lawsuits and Weaken Governance Oversight
data.description:
Coinbase Global, Inc. (COIN) has disclosed a new risk, in the Share Price & Shareholder Rights category.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An outside observer notes that Coinbase Global, Inc.’s bylaw requiring a shareholder or group to hold at least 3% of outstanding shares to initiate derivative actions materially raises the bar for pursuing claims against officers or directors. Combined with Texas law’s rejection of demand futility and the board’s 90‑day review window, this framework could significantly constrain shareholder litigation avenues.

This structure may deter smaller investors from challenging alleged misconduct, potentially weakening a key corporate governance safeguard that typically disciplines management behavior. It could also concentrate derivative enforcement power in a limited set of large holders whose interests may not always align with those of the broader shareholder base.

Overall, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on COIN stock based on 15 Buys, 2 Sells and 6 Holds.

To learn more about Coinbase Global, Inc.’s risk factors, click here.
